perturbation.original_text: The other Party will then have a period of fourteen (14) days from the date of the notice to indicate whether it also desires to extend the Term, on the terms and conditions set forth herein and if no such indication is made, the other Party will be deemed to have declined the offer to extend.
perturbation.changed_text: The other Party will then have a period of three (3) days from the date of the notice to indicate whether it also desires to extend the Term, on the terms and conditions set forth herein and if no such indication is made, the other Party will be deemed to have declined the offer to extend.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the response time for contract extension acceptance to 3 days could be unreasonably short and deemed unconscionable, especially if the party needs to consult legal counsel. This could be seen as a breach of good faith and fair dealing depending on jurisdiction.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Under contract law, every contract contains an implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.  Shortening the response time to an unreasonably short duration (3 days) could be interpreted as acting in bad faith, especially if it disadvantages the other party and prevents them from adequately assessing the situation or seeking advice. This could be viewed as an attempt to force a decline, breaching this implied covenant.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . [Last reviewed in January of 2023 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex COMMERCE business law contracts wex definitions business sectors legal education and practice Wex Toolbox

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the time for the other party to respond to a contract extension from 14 days to 3 days. The Law Explanation states that shortening the response time to an "unreasonably short duration (3 days) could be interpreted as acting in bad faith." The Perturbation Explanation supports this, arguing that a shorter time could be "unreasonably short and deemed unconscionable." Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines "good faith" as encompassing "honest dealing" and "observance of fair dealing standards." By reducing the response time to 3 days, the changed text creates a scenario where the other party may not have sufficient time to seek advice or adequately assess the situation, potentially violating the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.

perturbation.original_text: Naked shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon ten (10) days prior written notice to Wade in the event Wade fails to perform the Wade Services or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein (including the essence of this Agreement), and fails to cure same (if curable) within seven (7) days of receipt of written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: Naked shall have the right to terminate this Agreement upon two (2) days prior written notice to Wade in the event Wade fails to perform the Wade Services or breaches any other covenant or agreement set forth herein (including the essence of this Agreement), and fails to cure same (if curable) within one (1) day of receipt of written notice.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the notice and cure periods to two days and one day, respectively, may be considered unreasonably short and insufficient, potentially violating principles of fairness and adequate notice in contract law.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Breach of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing
perturbation.law_explanation: Providing only two days' notice of termination and one day to cure a breach could be deemed a violation of the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. This is because it does not allow the breaching party sufficient opportunity to understand the breach, take corrective action, or seek legal counsel, thereby undermining the spirit of the agreement.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: good faith | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you good faith Good faith is a broad term that’s used to encompass honest dealing. Depending on the exact setting, good faith may require an honest belief or purpose, faithful performance of duties , observance of fair dealing standards, or an absence of fraudulent intent . A fiduciary relationship creates a duty of good faith between the agent and the principal . The breach of this duty of good faith can lead to liability . Failure to act in good faith is known as bad faith and is generally considered to be a level of culpability greater than negligence . perturbation.justification: The modified text reduces the notice and cure periods from ten and seven days to two and one day, respectively. This creates an inconsistency that contradicts the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, as argued in the Law Explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 defines 'good faith' as encompassing 'observance of fair dealing standards.' By drastically shortening the time allowed for Wade to address a breach, the changed text undermines fair dealing and does not allow sufficient opportunity to understand the breach or take corrective action.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree to use commercially reasonable efforts to settle amicably any controversy, or claim arising out of the Agreement or any breach thereof through a dispute resolution process involving Wade and members from the senior management of Naked. If the parties do not otherwise agree, either party may present any unresolved dispute for arbitration in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association (the "Rules") then in effect. Such arbitration will be held in New York, NY. The arbitration will be by a single arbitrator chosen by the parties, provided that if the parties fail to agree and to appoint a single arbitrator within twenty (20) business days from the date that one of the parties has made a demand for arbitration, then the arbitrator will be chosen in accordance with the Rules.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree to use commercially reasonable efforts to settle amicably any controversy, or claim arising out of the Agreement or any breach thereof through a dispute resolution process involving Wade and members from the senior management of Naked. If the parties do not otherwise agree, either party may present any unresolved dispute for arbitration in accordance with the Commercial Arbitration Rules of the American Arbitration Association (the "Rules") then in effect. Such arbitration will be held in New York, NY. The arbitration will be by a single arbitrator chosen unilaterally by Naked, regardless of Wade's consent.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the requirement for mutual agreement on the arbitrator and granting Naked the unilateral right to choose the arbitrator violates the principle of fairness and impartiality in arbitration.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the Federal Arbitration Act (FAA) outlines the procedure for appointing arbitrators when the agreed-upon method fails. It emphasizes court intervention to ensure a fair and impartial selection process, which the modified clause directly contradicts by granting one party unilateral control over the arbitrator selection, potentially undermining the integrity and fairness of the arbitration process.

perturbation.justification: The original contract mandates mutual agreement on the arbitrator, ensuring impartiality. The changed text grants Naked unilateral control over arbitrator selection, removing the requirement for mutual agreement and potentially undermining the fairness of the arbitration process. This contradicts the principles of impartiality in arbitration, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. Although the scraped law snippets are not helpful, the Law Explanation states that FAA Section 5 underscores the importance of a fair and impartial arbitrator selection process, which is directly undermined by the modified clause.
